title: At the Clinic: Hearing Numbers and Dates
learningGoal: The learner will accurately extract specific numerical data (ages, phone fragments) and temporal data (days/months) from a rapid, native-speed English conversation, specifically discriminating between '-teen' and '-ty' sounds.
instructions: Listen to a receptionist talking to a new patient at a clinic. Pay very close attention to the questions she asks (What, When, How old) and the numbers the patient says. Is he saying 14 or 40? Listen carefully and answer the 8 questions.

contentItems.text: The audio is at native speed. If the numbers are too fast, use the slow playback button.

listeningGoal: Accurately parse auditory cues to isolate correct spelled-out integers and calendar dates.
audioScenario: A receptionist at a dentist office is registering a new patient, asking him standard intake questions.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Okay, Rohan. How old are you?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओके, रोहन। हाउ ओल्ड आर यू?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ठीक है, रोहन। आपकी उम्र क्या है?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Ah, okay. When is your birthday?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: आह, ओके। वेन इज़ योर बर्थडे?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: आह, ठीक है। आपका जन्मदिन कब है?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: My appointment is on Tuesday. Tuesday, August ten.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: माई अपॉइंटमेंट इज़ ऑन ट्यूसडे। ट्यूसडे, अगस्त टेन।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: मेरी अपॉइंटमेंट मंगलवार को है। मंगलवार, दस अगस्त।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: I live at fifty Park Road.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: आई लिव ऐट फिफ्टी पार्क रोड।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: मैं पचास पार्क रोड पर रहता हूँ।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Thank you, Rohan. Please sit down.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: थैंक यू, रोहन। प्लीज़ सिट डाउन।

audioTranscriptFull:
Receptionist: Hello. What is your name?
Rohan: My name is Rohan.
Receptionist: Okay, Rohan. How old are you?
Rohan: I am fourteen years old.
Receptionist: Forty?
Rohan: No, fourteen. One, four.
Receptionist: Ah, okay. When is your birthday?
Rohan: My birthday is in August.
Receptionist: When is your appointment?
Rohan: My appointment is on Tuesday. Tuesday, August ten.
Receptionist: Where do you live?
Rohan: I live at fifty Park Road.
Receptionist: Fifty or fifteen?
Rohan: Fifty. Five, zero.
Receptionist: Thank you, Rohan. Please sit down.

questionSet.questionText: What WH-word does the receptionist use to ask Rohan about his name?
In what month is Rohan's birthday?
What day of the week is his appointment?
What is the exact date (number) of his appointment in August?
What WH-word does the receptionist use to ask about his address (____ do you live)?
What is Rohan's house number? Listen carefully to the 'TY' vs 'TEEN'.
True or False: The receptionist perfectly heard all his numbers the first time without asking him to clarify.

scoringRules.partialCreditRules: No partial credit for Q2 and Q7. Distinguishing Teens from Tens is binary.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You successfully navigated a fast-paced conversation, accurately picking out numerical data and recognizing WH-question hooks.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Ensure you playback the audio on Slow mode. Listen to how Rohan strictly stresses 'Four-TEEN' but then stresses 'FIF-ty'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good ear! You caught most of the information. Always double check if you hear a stressed 'teen' at the end of a number or a soft 'ee' (ty).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Receptionist: Hello. What is your name? ... (matches full transcript)

hintText: If the number ends with an 'N' sound (Teen), it is a small number (13-19). If it ends in an 'EE' sound (Ty), it is a big number (30-90).
